title: sorbus bakonyensis
intro: Sorbus bakonyensis, also known as the Bakony whitebeam, is a rare species of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Bakony Mountains in Hungary. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Sorbus bakonyensis include its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making it effective in treating fever and pain. Herbal preparations of Sorbus bakonyensis typically involve infusions or decoctions of its bark, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including headaches and respiratory issues.
